agency: New York City Independent Budget Office (IBO)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting). IBO's own legacy domain ibo.nyc.ny.us 301-redirects to www.ibo.nyc.gov, a client-side SPA on the NYC.gov 'Content API v2' chassis (Akamai edge, nginx origin, Dynatrace/ruxit RUM, WebTrends analytics). Every route serves the same 36 KB shell; content is loaded by jQuery from apps.nyc.gov/content-api/v2 (custom-contents + nav). Confirmed that undocumented content API returns ~1,145 structured publications. Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'NYC Independent Budget Office (IBO)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 20 assets with columns; the task-suggested label 'Independent Budget Office (IBO)' returns zero — the real label is prefixed 'NYC'.
headline_findings: IBO gave up its own domain: the legacy ibo.nyc.ny.us now 301-redirects to www.ibo.nyc.gov — the 'Independent' Budget Office moved its public identity onto the shared NYC.gov chassis.

The site is a client-side SPA on the NYC.gov 'Content API v2' platform (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace/WebTrends). Every path returns the same shell; content is fetched by jQuery from a real but UNDOCUMENTED JSON API at apps.nyc.gov/content-api/v2 — a third distinct NYC.gov platform after NYCHA's Livesite.

IBO's product is analysis, and ~1,145 publications (reports, testimony, letters, budget options, explainers, interactives) already flow through that content API with structured metadata (title, date, topic, publication type, fiscal year, author) — but there is no documented, versioned, agent-native contract over it.

The 20 IBO Open Data datasets are WIDE and pivoted: one row per line-item, one column per fiscal year ('FY 1980' … 'FY 2020'). It is Excel-on-Socrata; a modern API would pivot to long-form (series, lineItem, fiscalYear, value).

As a reference/analysis agency, IBO has NO citizen transaction — no payments, permits, or applications. Its single interactive input is 'Ask IBO' (a data/analysis request), today a web form / email to info@ibo.nyc.gov with no API. That is the net-new WRITE surface (DataRequest).
Data-viz is outsourced: interactives embed Airtable, ArcGIS Online (ibomap.maps.arcgis.com), and Infogram; site search is a Google Custom Search. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: Backs the SPA. Returns ~1,145 IBO publications with structured metadata (Title, Publication Date, Topics, Publication Type, Fiscal Year, Author) plus a /nav/ibo tree. Real machine-readable API but no OpenAPI, no versioned consumer contract.
20 IBO datasets under label 'NYC Independent Budget Office (IBO)'. SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. Fiscal time-series, mostly WIDE (one column per fiscal year).
Client-side jQuery/Bootstrap SPA; every route returns the same 36 KB shell. Formerly ibo.nyc.ny.us (301-redirect).
Map interactives embedded via CSP frame-src.
Some IBO interactives are Airtable embeds — data outside IBO's own contract.
On-site search is a Google Custom Search Engine — search rented, not owned.
ruxitagentjs injection + webtrends_v10.js on every page.

fruit.name: IBO Publications (reports, testimony, letters, budget options, explainers, interactives)
fruit.notes: IBO's actual product. Already served with structured metadata by apps.nyc.gov/content-api/v2/custom-contents/ibo/IBO-Publications, but no documented/versioned/agent-native contract and no Open Data twin. Types: Report (688), Interactive (201), Testimony (158), Letter (79), Press Release (7), Budget Options (5), Explainer (4), Dataset (3).

fruit.name: Fiscal data tables (catalog)
fruit.notes: The 20 IBO Socrata assets as one uniform catalog rather than 20 opaque four-by-fours. Most are WIDE (one column per fiscal year).

fruit.name: Revenue & spending, capital, debt, positions (fiscal series)
fruit.notes: Eight parallel WIDE tables (one column per FY 1980–2020). Modeled as long-form (series, lineItem, fiscalYear, value). Covers the RevenueForecast and CapitalBudget entity hints.

fruit.name: School spending & education indicators
fruit.notes: Per-category school spending by year (WIDE, one column per year 1990–2017) plus the Education Indicators interactive. The SchoolBudgetData entity hint.

fruit.name: Tax & income distribution by AGI band
opendata_match: Personal Income by AGI Range ipc3-2nbm
Income by Type and AGI Range gffu-ps8j
Tax Credits/EITC by AGI Range nwet-nc6h
fruit.notes: Income, liability, and credits keyed on income group; plus tax-revenue lines by tax type. IBO's most-viewed datasets (Income by Type, ~12k views).

fruit.name: COVID-19 / federal stimulus spending trackers
opendata_match: COVID-19 Spending by Agency by Expense duk5-k5fk
fruit.notes: IBO's stimulus/COVID budget-and-spending trackers — the closest thing to live, current data in the corpus.

fruit.name: Budget 101 / NYC Budget Resources
fruit.notes: The 'NYC Budget Resources' nav section (Budget 101 and explainers) — reference content served through the same content API.

fruit.name: Ask IBO (submit a data / analysis request)
fruit.notes: IBO's ONE citizen transaction — a resident, journalist, or Council staffer asks a fiscal question. Today a web form / email to info@ibo.nyc.gov with no API and no Open Data twin. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain (there is no payment/permit/application to model).

meta.platform: NYC.gov Content API v2 SPA (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace/WebTrends); off legacy ibo.nyc.ny.us; 20 Socrata datasets; Airtable/ArcGIS/Infogram embeds
meta.netnew: DataRequest — an API for the 'Ask IBO' data/analysis request, today a web form / email only
meta.tagline: NYC's nonpartisan budget watchdog already runs a real content API and 20 open datasets, but neither has a documented, agent-native contract.
meta.headline: IBO's analysis flows through an undocumented NYC.gov content API and wide, Excel-shaped fiscal tables; the fruit is formalizing both into one owned contract plus the missing 'Ask IBO' write surface.

scorecard_notes.platform: Modern-ish NYC.gov Content API v2 SPA, but client-side jQuery/Bootstrap; migrated off its own legacy .ny.us domain.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: A real public JSON content API exists (apps.nyc.gov/content-api/v2) but is undocumented, unversioned, and not offered as a consumer contract.
scorecard_notes.openData: 20 machine-readable Socrata datasets, but WIDE/pivoted, historical, and awkward to consume; agency label is 'NYC ...(IBO)'.
scorecard_notes.coreData: IBO's core product (~1,145 publications) is well-published via the content API, but the underlying analytical data (forecasts, models) stays in PDFs/interactives.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: None — Ask IBO is a form/email; IBO has no transactional API surface at all.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface: no MCP, no OpenAPI, undocumented content API, search rented to Google.
scorecard_notes.ownership: First-party over its publications and datasets on shared NYC.gov infra, but data-viz and search outsourced to Airtable/ArcGIS/Infogram/Google.
